title: Non-Record: Training on the Apple Neural Engine
author: garindean

data.description: Grouped-query attention with 8 query heads and 4 KV heads.
Uses SwiGLU feed-forward blocks instead of relu^2.
Embedding weights are tied to the classifier/output weights.
Uses rotary positional embeddings with base 10000.
Applies DeepNet-style residual scaling with alpha = 1/sqrt(2*N_layers).
Training and selected backward computations are accelerated on Apple's Neural Engine, with CPU handling unsupported operations, weight gradients, and optimizer updates.

parameters.hardware: Apple M4 Pro Neural Engine + CPU

data.description: Loss is multiplied by 256 before backpropagation and divided out before weight updates to mitigate FP16 underflow.

novel_contributions: Uses the Apple Neural Engine as the primary accelerator for transformer forward passes and some backward computations.
Implements a dynamic weight pipeline using IOSurface shared memory to avoid recompiling ANE graphs after weight updates.
Splits training across ANE and CPU, with ANE handling matmuls and dx while CPU handles masking, normalization, gradients, and optimization.
Applies loss scaling to address FP16-only compute underflow on the ANE.
Provides a compressed int8+zlib artifact that can be evaluated without ANE hardware.
